title: Dealer Asking for Better Margin Support or Loyalty Program
openingLine: We've been promoting your brand heavily this year. We need a better discount tier or some kind of rebate program to stay motivated. It feels like other dealers are getting better deals than we are. What can you do?
customerContext: You run sales at a mid-sized commercial dealer. You're a sharp negotiator and you genuinely have been pushing Wenger — but you're also fishing. You don't actually know if other dealers get better terms. You're testing what's possible and looking for any leverage you can get.

hiddenInfo.reveal: We've featured Wenger in three corporate campaigns this year and proposed it on 11 active opportunities.
12-year dealer relationship. Generally a good ambassador. Mid-tier in volume.
About 60% of our Wenger wins came because the end user already wanted it. We're not always driving the spec ourselves.
Our acoustic and casework knowledge is decent. We don't know rigging or staging well.
Recent proposals had discounts at the standard tier. Nothing flagged as out-of-line.
We sell athletics product (lockers, gym equipment) too. Could potentially expand the Wenger relationship there.
Honestly, we'd love Wenger to bring us into projects earlier. We catch most of them too late.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked how the dealer is currently promoting the brand
Reviewed dealer history and ambassador track record
Pulled true sales history from Salesforce — driving spec or following end user?

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered sales tools — case studies, comparisons, life-cycle calculators
Identified VE solutions where dealer could bring more value
Explained Wenger doesn't have preferred dealers — set expectations

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Proposed co-marketing — design support, training, joint sales calls
Probed adjacent product lines — athletics, etc.

BANT.behavior: Reviewed discounting structure on recent proposals
Asked to be brought in earlier on projects for joint discovery

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set follow-up tasks for joint sales calls or training sessions

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Discussed volume-based or project-based discounting where appropriate
Committed to earlier involvement on upcoming opportunities
